hypothesis: Introducing aromatic substitutions at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2 and TM5, while avoiding off-target interactions. This should increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing affinity for SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective for generating strong SSTR2 binders, but off-target interactions at conserved positions are limiting selectivity. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to push Δmargin consistently above 0.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to mediate conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore at positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14.
Introducing charged or hydrophobic residues at positions 1/2 and small, non-polar residues at 5/6/11/12 can disrupt conserved off-target interactions and improve SSTR2 selectivity.
